title: Chile-Lime Chicken Wraps with Avocado-Cilantro Sauce & Jicama Slaw
description: Charred chile-lime chicken strips, bright avocado-cilantro sauce, crunchy jicama-radish slaw, and fresh pico — all in a sprouted-grain tortilla. No dairy, big flavor.

ingredients.name: sprouted grain or whole wheat tortillas
kosher salt, for sauce, slaw, and pico

instructions.text: Marinade: whisk together avocado oil, ancho chile powder, smoked paprika, cumin, oregano, garlic, lime zest and juice, orange juice, and salt. Add chicken strips and toss to coat every piece. Cover and marinate at room temp 30 minutes, or up to 4 hours refrigerated.

Avocado-cilantro sauce: blend avocado, cilantro, jalapeño, garlic, lime juice, olive oil, and a pinch of salt until completely smooth. Add cold water a tablespoon at a time until drizzle-able but still clings to a spoon. Should be bright and limey. Refrigerate until ready to serve.

Jicama-radish slaw: toss julienned jicama, sliced radishes, and half the red onion with lime juice and a pinch of salt. Let sit at least 10 minutes — the lime softens the bite while keeping the crunch.
Quick pico: combine diced tomatoes, remaining red onion, lime juice, and a pinch of salt. Add a few chopped cilantro stems if you have extra.

Sear the chicken: heat a large stainless or cast iron pan over high heat until very hot. Lay strips in a single layer. Sear undisturbed 3 minutes until deeply browned, flip, and cook 2–3 minutes more to 165°F internal. Rest 5 minutes on a board — do not skip or the wraps get soggy.
Warm tortillas in a dry pan over medium heat, about 30 seconds per side, until pliable with a few toasted spots. Stack under a towel to keep warm.
Assemble: spread a generous layer of avocado-cilantro sauce down each tortilla. Add chicken strips, then slaw, then pico. Roll tight burrito-style with ends tucked, and slice on a hard diagonal.

notes:
The avocado-cilantro sauce keeps well refrigerated for a few days — make a full batch regardless of serving size.

To batch-cook for weekday lunches, double just the chicken (2 lb) and keep everything else at this 2-serving size. That gives you dinner plus 2–3 lunch portions without excess slaw.
